description: You chose to spare the bandit's life instead of turning them over to the guard.

consequences.description: The bandit you spared intercepts a thief trying to rob you in the market, repaying the life you gave them.
Word of your mercy spreads through the underground — criminals now hesitate before attacking you, unsure if you'll show the same compassion or demand justice.
The spared bandit arrives at the gates of the besieged town, leading a company of reformed outlaws to fight at your side — the mercy you showed has become a rallying banner.

description: You stole the sacred relic from the Temple of the Silver Moon.

consequences.description: A temple acolyte recognises the faint divine aura clinging to your belongings and confronts you in the street, demanding the relic's return.
The moon's blessing no longer protects your camp at night — you are plagued by vivid nightmares of judgment, and your companions wake unrested.
The High Priestess of the Silver Moon places a sacred bounty on your head; clerics across the realm now refuse to heal you, recognising the temple's mark upon your soul.

description: You accepted the Duke's offer of political alliance and swore fealty to the noble house.

consequences.description: Common folk eye you with suspicion in taverns and villages — to them, you are now one of the oppressors, regardless of your intentions.
The Duke sends a retinue of soldiers to escort you, lending legitimacy but also making stealth and independent action nearly impossible.
A rival noble family, enemies of the Duke, now considers you a threat; an assassin bearing their crest is found dead near your lodgings, and whispers of a coming purge fill the court.

description: You risked your life to pull the child from the burning building.

consequences.description: The child's parent, a skilled blacksmith, offers to forge or repair your equipment free of charge — 'You gave me back my world; let me give you steel.'
Bards in the region begin singing of 'The Flame-Scarred Hero' — strangers recognise you and some offer aid, though others wonder if you seek glory for yourself.
The child, now a young apprentice mage, seeks you out years later to return the favour at the darkest hour — a shield of arcane fire stands between you and death.

description: You betrayed your companion's trust, revealing their secret to a mutual enemy.

consequences.description: Your former companion sends a cold letter: 'I know what you did. We are no longer friends.' The ink smells faintly of poison — a warning, not yet an attack.
Other allies begin to keep secrets from you; information flows less freely, and NPCs you once trusted now hedge their words and avoid being alone with you.
Your betrayed companion has risen in power and now commands a faction that opposes you at every turn — at a critical negotiation, they reveal *your* secrets to the room.
